articles.title: HOW TO SOFT DISABLE UNUSED FEATURES
articles.summary: When you've got billions of page views per month, unused features have a price - whether that's cloud resources or carbon emissions. What can you do about it?

articles.title: >> Read the article on the Adevinta tech blog
WHY GOV.UK'S EXIT THIS PAGE COMPONENT DOESN'T USE THE ESCAPE KEY
articles.summary: GOV.UK's Exit this Page component uses the Shift key instead of the Escape key for its shortcut function due to several technical limitations. The Escape key is not a reliable option because it's commonly used by browsers and operating systems for other functions, and its use is not always considered a user interaction by browsers. Other keys like Control or Alt have their own challenges, so Shift was chosen as the least flawed option.

articles.title: JOHN CARMACK ON INLINED CODE
articles.summary: Doom co-creator John Carmack advocates for inlining code as a way to improve code clarity and reduce bugs. Inlining helps programmers become aware of all the code being executed, eliminates the possibility of functions being called from unintended places, and reduces the risk of bugs stemming from state changes. As an example, aerospace software uses inlining for reliability.

articles.title: YOU DON'T KNOW JACK ABOUT BANDWIDTH
articles.summary: Internet performance issues are often caused by latency, not insufficient bandwidth. This latency is exacerbated by outdated router software that leads to buffering and congestion, especially during video calls and other high-demand activities. There are various software solutions to address these latency problems at the ISP level that have proven effective in the real world.

articles.title: WHY THE ISO FORMAT HAS TO DIE
articles.summary: The ISO format is arguably outdated and should be retired. The image format is better because it's simpler and easier to use.

articles.title: WARM HANDOFFS
articles.summary: Warm handoffs are when questions are redirected in chat channels to the appropriate team and channel. By involving the initial contact in the handoff, providing context, and ensuring the requester's continued involvement, warm handoffs create a more efficient and collaborative environment. An organization that encourages warm handoffs ends up collaborating better over time.

articles.title: HOW TO MAKE PRODUCT GIVE A SHIT ABOUT YOUR ARCHITECTURE PROPOSAL
articles.summary: Software engineers should advocate for robust, well-designed solutions, even if they require significant time and resources. It's important to learn to negotiate scope and time with product teams while maintaining quality standards.

articles.title: YOU KNOW WHAT'S FUN? NEW REDIS TOOLS.
articles.summary: See our latest tools and releases at Redis Released: Worldwide. [11]Whether you're a startup or enterprise company, teams like yours are using Redis to overcome data fragmentation, AI complexities, and scaling models. And they're breaking down how at this free virtual event on November 6 and 7. Save your spot [11].

articles.title: PLACEHOLDERJS
articles.summary: PlaceholderJS is a free, lightweight service for developers that provides simple image placeholders with customizable size, text, text color, and background color.

articles.title: HULY PLATFORM
articles.summary: Huly Platform is a framework designed to accelerate the development of business applications. It includes several applications such as Chat, Project Management, CRM, HRM, and ATS.

articles.title: HOW FIRESHIP BECAME YOUTUBE'S FAVORITE PROGRAMMER
articles.summary: Fireship is a popular YouTube channel that has over 3 million subscribers through its unique style of fast-paced, humorous, and meme-filled videos explaining programming concepts. The channel's strategy focuses on evergreen videos, such as its "X in 100 Seconds" series, and “trend-jacking,” with its "The Code Report" series.

articles.title: REACT AT META CONNECT 2024
articles.summary: Meta Connect 2024 showcased several projects built using React and React Native. React and React Native help teams develop for Meta Quest, Instagram, Facebook, the Meta Horizon mobile app, and the Meta Horizon Store. The Meta Spatial Editor, a desktop app for creating 3D experiences, was built using React Native for Desktop.

articles.title: BUILDING OFFLINE-FIRST MOBILE APPS WITH SUPABASE, FLUTTER, AND BRICK
articles.summary: Brick is a Flutter data management tool that simplifies building offline-first mobile apps with Supabase. It handles data synchronization between Supabase and local caches and it offers a single repository to interact with data. This allows for easy data fetching and caching while also providing features like reactivity and upserting for efficient data updates.

articles.title: THE INTERNET ARCHIVE IS UNDER ATTACK, WITH A BREACH REVEALING INFO FOR 31 MILLION ACCOUNTS
articles.summary: The Internet Archive suffered a data breach affecting 31 million accounts and is currently under a DDoS attack, with the perpetrators defacing the website using a JavaScript library and disrupting its services with popups.

articles.title: THE IMPENDING FATE OF THE .IO DOMAIN
articles.summary: The .io domain might face dissolution due to political shifts, specifically the UK's decision to return the Chagos Islands to Mauritius.

articles.title: I WASTED A DAY ON CSS SELECTOR PERFORMANCE TO MAKE A WEBSITE LOAD 2MS FASTER
articles.summary: This developer spent a day optimizing CSS selectors to improve website performance, only to discover that the perceived improvement was the result of profiling tools - the actual performance gain was negligible.

articles.title: ‘THE COMMUNITY IS IN CHAOS:' WORDPRESS.ORG NOW REQUIRES YOU DENOUNCE AFFILIATION WITH WP ENGINE TO LOG IN
articles.summary: WordPress co-creator Matt Mullenweg has escalated his dispute with WP Engine, forcing WordPress.org users to denounce any affiliation with the hosting platform before logging in.

articles.title: GRATEFUL: COLORS IN CONSOLE.LOG()
articles.summary: This developer used CSS styling to visually differentiate console.log statements and debug a UI state issue.
